customer_name: Vermont Museum of Natural History Inc.

citations.narrative:
The facility has a documented contingency plan, however an annual review still has not been conducted since May of
2024. An annual review of the contingency plan helps ensure that any needed changes are made and communicated to
all personnel that have a role or responsibilities in the plan to provide for the humane handling, treatment, transportation,
housing and care of the animals in the event of an emergency or disaster. The facility must ensure that the contingency
plan is reviewed on at least an annual basis and must also maintain documentation of those reviews. The facility must
correct this issue.

Multiple ends of jagged wire was observed protruding into the skunk enclosure. This was observed along the bottom
portion where the chain link wire meets the wooden side panels. This issue could potentially cause injury to the animal.
Animal enclosures must be kept in good repair in order to secure the animals and minimize any potential risk of injury.
The facility must correct this issue.
*******Correct by 4/22/26

The facility is still storing leftover food items in the freezer which is used to store bird food items. This material is collected
from the bird enclosures as part of the process in order to monitor consumption. This consists leftover food items, feathers
and waste debris. Animal waste and food residue can potentially lead to contamination of the animals diet, and these
items not intended for food must not be kept in food storage areas or food freezers. The facility must correct this issue.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
n

narrative:
2.134(b)  Repeat
   
Contingency planning. 
The facility has a documented contingency plan, however an annual review still has not been conducted since May of 
2024. An annual review of the contingency plan helps ensure that any needed changes are made and communicated to 
all personnel that have a role or responsibilities in the plan to provide for the humane handling, treatment, transportation, 
housing and care of the animals in the event of an emergency or disaster. The facility must ensure that the contingency 
plan is reviewed on at least an annual basis and must also maintain documentation of those reviews. The facility must 
correct this issue. 
3.125(a) 
   
Facilities, general. 
Multiple ends of jagged wire was observed protruding into the skunk enclosure. This was observed along the bottom 
portion where the chain link wire meets the wooden side panels. This issue could potentially cause injury to the animal. 
Animal enclosures must be kept in good repair in order to secure the animals and minimize any potential risk of injury. 
The facility must correct this issue.  
*******Correct by 4/22/26 
3.150(e)  Repeat
   
Facilities, general. 
The facility is still storing leftover food items in the freezer which is used to store bird food items. This material is collected 
from the bird enclosures as part of the process in order to monitor consumption. This consists leftover food items, feathers 
and waste debris. Animal waste and food residue can potentially lead to contamination of the animals diet, and these 
items not intended for food must not be kept in food storage areas or food freezers. The facility must correct this issue. 
 
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representative.
